title: abc9: uniquify blackboxes like whiteboxes
body:
`abc9` will uniquify parameterised whiteboxes (i.e. create a unique module with parameter values constant-propagated) but not blackboxes. This is an oversight resolved here.

Whiteboxes would be typically preferred so that `abc9` can optimise the box away or short it if necessary. It won't do that for a blackbox, however, it will still sweep that box away if it has a dangling/redundant output (making this not a completely bulletproof equivalent to `(* keep *)` which hides it from `abc9` entirely -- but also means it will not be considered for timing-aware mapping).

CC: @Ravenslofty
